title: HCI Topology Change Auditing and Unauthorized Expansion Detection
exclusions: Does not replace UC-19.2.10 version compliance baselines, UC-19.2.26 VxRail LCM compliance workflows, UC-19.2.16 configured failure-domain risk tables, UC-19.2.4 recommendation acceptance analytics, or UC-19.2.17 disk-group health signaling. Not aimed at pure Kubernetes node autoscaler churn decoupled from HCI membership. Does not parse guest VM provisioning alone without a cluster-level topology signal. Omits mainframe and non-x86 estates. Does not certify hardware support entitlement; it only surfaces mismatches for human review.
evidence: Scheduled saved search hci_uc_19_2_18_topology_governance with version-controlled lookups/hci_change_governance.csv and lookups/change_tickets_24h.csv, weekly CSV exports of the final table to a restricted evidence index, and ticket deep links plus vCenter, Prism, HX Connect, or Windows Admin Center screenshots for each critical row.

controlTest.positiveScenario: In a lab index, index one vmware:vc:event HostAddedEvent for cluster_id LAB-HCI with actor local_admin absent from approved_actor_pattern, omit any change_tickets_24h row for that cluster, and run the saved search across a window containing the event; expect unauthorized_change_count_24h of at least one, non_approved_actor_count_24h of at least one, severity critical, and a positive oldest_unauthorized_age_minutes after the stats stage. Add a DatastoreExpandedEvent in the same window without a ticket to assert disk_group_expand_no_ticket_indicator equals one.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Populate change_tickets_24h with a row whose change_window_start and change_window_end bracket the same HostAddedEvent within the core window, set hci_change_governance approved_actor_pattern to match the service account on the event, align expected_node_count with a following vmware:topology:host_inventory snapshot, and confirm unauthorized_change_count_24h returns zero, cmdb_topology_drift_count stays zero, and severity falls to info unless another cluster in the multisearch still breaches.

dataSources: VMware vCenter via Splunk Add-on for VMware vSphere (Splunkbase 5380): sourcetype vmware:vc:event for HostAddedEvent, HostRemovedEvent, EnteredMaintenanceModeEvent, ExitedMaintenanceModeEvent, DatastoreExpandedEvent, EnteredStandbyModeEvent; scheduled PowerCLI or Universal Forwarder JSON for Get-VMHost and Get-VsanDiskGroup snapshots as sourcetypes vmware:topology:host_inventory and vmware:vsan:disk_group_inventory. Nutanix Prism Element via Splunk_TA_nutanix (5076): sourcetype nutanix:audit for cluster expand, add_node, remove_node, enter_maintenance from ncli or Prism UI; custom HEC poller to REST /PrismGateway/services/rest/v2.0/cluster and /v2.0/hosts as nutanix:cluster:topology_snapshot and nutanix:host:inventory. Cisco HyperFlex: HX Connect audit from /rest/audit/events as hyperflex:audit:event for expand_cluster, add_node, remove_node, enter_maintenance; optional ucsm:audit for UCS Manager blade insertion or removal. Azure Stack HCI and Windows Failover Cluster: Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows for Microsoft-Windows-FailoverClustering Operational and Admin channels; scheduled PowerShell export of Get-ClusterLog -Detailed excerpts plus Add-ClusterNode, Remove-ClusterNode, Suspend-ClusterNode style events as azure_stack_hci:cluster:event and periodic Get-ClusterNode counts as azure_stack_hci:cluster:topology_snapshot. Change correlation: customer-curated lookup change_tickets_24h (cluster_id, ticket_id, change_window_start, change_window_end, requestor) and governance lookup hci_change_governance (cluster_id, vendor, owner_team, target_max_out_of_window, approved_actor_pattern, expected_node_count, change_window_lookup_name, change_freeze_until).
app: `Splunk Add-on for VMware vSphere` ([Splunkbase 5380](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/5380)); `Splunk_TA_nutanix` ([Splunkbase 5076](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/5076)); Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows for Failover Clustering channels; custom HEC collectors for HX Connect audit, Prism topology snapshots, PowerCLI inventory exports; optional Cisco UCS TA for ucsm:audit
spl:
`comment("UC-19.2.18 HCI Topology Change Auditing and Unauthorized Expansion Detection. Tunables: hci_change_governance.csv (target_max_out_of_window default 0, approved_actor_pattern regex, expected_node_count, change_window_lookup_name, change_freeze_until ISO8601 UTC). Owner: owner_team. Freeze: future change_freeze_until forces severity info. Lookups: hci_change_governance, change_tickets_24h via join subsearches only. Replace index=hci with indexes your SO approves.")`
| multisearch
    [ search (index=hci OR index=vmware) (sourcetype="vmware:vc:event" OR sourcetype="vmware:topology:host_inventory" OR sourcetype="vmware:vsan:disk_group_inventory") earliest=-25h@h latest=@h
      | eval cluster_id=toString(coalesce(cluster_id, clusterId, cluster_name, clusterName, computeResource, compute_resource, dest_cluster_id, "UNKNOWN_CLUSTER"))
      | eval et_raw=toString(coalesce(Type, type, eventType, EventType, operation, Operation, ""))
      | eval et=lower(et_raw)
      | eval is_snap=if(match(sourcetype, "topology:host_inventory|vsan:disk_group_inventory"), 1, 0)
      | eval topology_evt=case(is_snap=1, "snapshot", match(et, "hostadded|host_added"), "node_add", match(et, "hostremoved|host_removed"), "node_remove", match(et, "enteredmaintenance|enter.*maintenance"), "maint_enter", match(et, "exitedmaintenance|exitedstandby"), "maint_exit", match(et, "datastoreexpanded|datastore_expand"), "disk_expand", match(et, "enteredstandby|standby"), "standby_toggle", match(et, "remove.*disk|disk.*remove|diskgroup"), "disk_group_change", true(), "other_topology")
      | eval actor=toString(coalesce(userName, user_name, username, UserName, account, src_user, user, subject, "unknown"))
      | eval event_id=if(is_snap=1, md5(cluster_id."|".sourcetype."|".tostring(_time)), toString(coalesce(event_id, eventId, chainedId, md5(cluster_id."|".et_raw."|".tostring(_time)))))
      | eval snapshot_node_count=if(is_snap=1, tonumber(tostring(coalesce(live_node_count, host_count, hostCount, num_hosts, total_hosts, node_count, NodeCount, "0")), 10), null())
      | eval vendor="vmware"
      | where cluster_id!="UNKNOWN_CLUSTER" AND (is_snap=1 OR match(et, "hostadded|hostremoved|maintenance|standby|datastoreexpanded|disk"))
      | stats latest(_time) AS _time latest(actor) AS actor latest(topology_evt) AS topology_evt latest(snapshot_node_count) AS snapshot_node_count latest(et_raw) AS et_raw BY cluster_id event_id vendor ]
    [ search (index=hci OR index=nutanix) (sourcetype="nutanix:audit" OR sourcetype="nutanix:cluster:topology_snapshot" OR sourcetype="nutanix:host:inventory") earliest=-25h@h latest=@h
      | eval cluster_id=toString(coalesce(cluster_id, clusterId, cluster_name, clusterName, cluster_uuid, clusterUuid, "UNKNOWN_CLUSTER"))
      | eval op=lower(toString(coalesce(operation, action, command, audit_action, event_type, " ")))
      | eval is_snap=if(match(sourcetype, "topology_snapshot|host:inventory"), 1, 0)
      | eval topology_evt=case(is_snap=1, "snapshot", match(op, "cluster_expand|expand_cluster|add_node|node_add"), "node_add", match(op, "remove_node|node_remove"), "node_remove", match(op, "enter_maintenance|maintenance"), "maint_enter", match(op, "exit_maintenance"), "maint_exit", match(op, "disk|storage.*expand"), "disk_expand", true(), "other_topology")
      | eval actor=toString(coalesce(userName, user_name, username, UserName, user_id, request_user, "unknown"))
      | eval event_id=if(is_snap=1, md5(cluster_id."|".sourcetype."|".tostring(_time)), toString(coalesce(event_id, eventId, audit_id, md5(cluster_id."|".op."|".tostring(_time)))))
      | eval snapshot_node_count=if(is_snap=1, tonumber(tostring(coalesce(num_hosts, host_count, node_total, entity_count, "0")), 10), null())
      | eval vendor="nutanix"
      | where cluster_id!="UNKNOWN_CLUSTER"
      | stats latest(_time) AS _time latest(actor) AS actor latest(topology_evt) AS topology_evt latest(snapshot_node_count) AS snapshot_node_count latest(op) AS et_raw BY cluster_id event_id vendor ]
    [ search (index=hci OR index=cisco) (sourcetype="hyperflex:audit:event" OR sourcetype="ucsm:audit") earliest=-25h@h latest=@h
      | eval cluster_id=toString(coalesce(cluster_id, clusterId, cluster_name, clusterName, ucs_cluster, "UNKNOWN_CLUSTER"))
      | eval op=lower(toString(coalesce(operation, action, event_type, eventType, audit_type, " ")))
      | eval topology_evt=case(sourcetype="ucsm:audit" AND match(op, "remove|decom|disassociate"), "node_remove", sourcetype="ucsm:audit" AND match(op, "insert|add|associate"), "node_add", match(op, "expand_cluster"), "node_add", match(op, "add_node"), "node_add", match(op, "remove_node"), "node_remove", match(op, "enter_maintenance"), "maint_enter", match(op, "exit_maintenance"), "maint_exit", true(), "other_topology")
      | eval actor=toString(coalesce(userName, user_name, username, UserName, dn, subject, "unknown"))
      | eval event_id=toString(coalesce(event_id, eventId, md5(cluster_id."|".sourcetype."|".op."|".tostring(_time))))
      | eval snapshot_node_count=null()
      | eval vendor="cisco_hyperflex"
      | where cluster_id!="UNKNOWN_CLUSTER" AND (sourcetype="hyperflex:audit:event" OR match(op, "insert|remove|add|decom|expand|maintenance"))
      | stats latest(_time) AS _time latest(actor) AS actor latest(topology_evt) AS topology_evt latest(snapshot_node_count) AS snapshot_node_count latest(op) AS et_raw BY cluster_id event_id vendor ]
    [ search (index=hci OR index=microsoft) (sourcetype="azure_stack_hci:cluster:event" OR sourcetype="azure_stack_hci:cluster:topology_snapshot") earliest=-25h@h latest=@h
      | eval cluster_id=toString(coalesce(cluster_name, clusterName, cluster_id, failover_cluster_name, "UNKNOWN_CLUSTER"))
      | eval op=lower(toString(coalesce(Operation, operation, event_type, EventCode, message_id, " ")))
      | eval is_snap=if(sourcetype="azure_stack_hci:cluster:topology_snapshot", 1, 0)
      | eval topology_evt=case(is_snap=1, "snapshot", match(op, "add-clusternode|add_clusternode"), "node_add", match(op, "remove-clusternode|remove_clusternode"), "node_remove", match(op, "suspend-clusternode|pause"), "maint_enter", match(op, "resume-clusternode"), "maint_exit", match(op, "expand|storage"), "disk_expand", true(), "other_topology")
      | eval actor=toString(coalesce(UserName, user_name, username, subject, "unknown"))
      | eval event_id=if(is_snap=1, md5(cluster_id."|snap|".tostring(_time)), toString(coalesce(EventRecordID, record_id, md5(cluster_id."|".op."|".tostring(_time)))))
      | eval snapshot_node_count=if(is_snap=1, tonumber(tostring(coalesce(node_count, NodeCount, cluster_node_count, "0")), 10), null())
      | eval vendor="azure_stack_hci"
      | where cluster_id!="UNKNOWN_CLUSTER"
      | stats latest(_time) AS _time latest(actor) AS actor latest(topology_evt) AS topology_evt latest(snapshot_node_count) AS snapshot_node_count latest(op) AS et_raw BY cluster_id event_id vendor ]
| eval is_topology_change=if(topology_evt!="snapshot" AND topology_evt!="other_topology", 1, 0)
| fillnull value=0 is_topology_change
| eventstats latest(eval(if(topology_evt="snapshot", snapshot_node_count, null()))) AS live_inventory_nodes BY cluster_id vendor
| join type=left max=0 cluster_id vendor
    [| inputlookup hci_change_governance
     | eval cluster_id=toString(cluster_id)
     | eval vendor=toString(vendor)
     | eval owner_team=toString(owner_team)
     | eval target_max_out_of_window=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(target_max_out_of_window, "0")), 10)
     | eval approved_actor_pattern=toString(coalesce(approved_actor_pattern, "^(svc_|admin_ops_team)"))
     | eval expected_node_count=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(expected_node_count, "-1")), 10)
     | eval change_window_lookup_name=toString(coalesce(change_window_lookup_name, "change_windows"))
     | eval change_freeze_until=toString(coalesce(change_freeze_until, ""))
     | fields cluster_id vendor owner_team target_max_out_of_window approved_actor_pattern expected_node_count change_window_lookup_name change_freeze_until ]
| join type=left max=0 cluster_id
    [| inputlookup change_tickets_24h
     | eval cluster_id=toString(cluster_id)
     | eval tws=coalesce(strptime(change_window_start, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S%z"), strptime(change_window_start, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"), strptime(change_window_start, "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"), strptime(change_window_start, "%Y-%m-%d"))
     | eval twe=coalesce(strptime(change_window_end, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S%z"), strptime(change_window_end, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"), strptime(change_window_end, "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"), strptime(change_window_end, "%Y-%m-%d"))
     | fields cluster_id ticket_id tws twe requestor ]
| eval has_matching_ticket=if(isnotnull(tws) AND isnotnull(twe) AND _time>=(tws-7200) AND _time<=(twe+7200), 1, 0)
| eval in_core_window=if(isnotnull(tws) AND isnotnull(twe) AND _time>=tws AND _time<=twe, 1, 0)
| eval is_unauthorized=if((is_topology_change=1) AND (has_matching_ticket=0), 1, 0)
| eval is_out_window=if((is_topology_change=1) AND (has_matching_ticket=1) AND (in_core_window=0), 1, 0)
| eval actor_ok=if(match(actor, approved_actor_pattern), 1, 0)
| eval is_non_approved_actor=if((is_topology_change=1) AND (actor_ok=0), 1, 0)
| eval maint_hit=if((topology_evt="maint_enter") AND ((has_matching_ticket=0) OR (in_core_window=0)), 1, 0)
| eval disk_hit=if((topology_evt="disk_expand") AND (has_matching_ticket=0), 1, 0)
| sort 0 + cluster_id vendor _time
| streamstats window=10000 current=t global=f min(eval(if(is_unauthorized=1, _time, null()))) AS sts_min_unauth_ts BY cluster_id vendor
| streamstats window=200 current=t global=f count AS ev_seq BY cluster_id vendor
| eventstats max(eval(if(is_topology_change=1 AND expected_node_count>=0 AND isnotnull(live_inventory_nodes) AND live_inventory_nodes!=expected_node_count, 1, 0))) AS cmdb_topology_drift_count BY cluster_id vendor
| stats sum(is_topology_change) AS topology_changes_24h sum(is_unauthorized) AS unauthorized_change_count_24h sum(is_out_window) AS out_of_window_change_count_24h sum(is_non_approved_actor) AS non_approved_actor_count_24h max(maint_hit) AS maint_mode_no_window_indicator max(disk_hit) AS disk_group_expand_no_ticket_indicator max(cmdb_topology_drift_count) AS cmdb_topology_drift_count latest(owner_team) AS owner_team latest(target_max_out_of_window) AS target_max_out_of_window latest(approved_actor_pattern) AS approved_actor_pattern latest(change_freeze_until) AS change_freeze_until latest(sts_min_unauth_ts) AS oldest_unauth_epoch BY cluster_id vendor
| fillnull value=0 topology_changes_24h unauthorized_change_count_24h out_of_window_change_count_24h non_approved_actor_count_24h maint_mode_no_window_indicator disk_group_expand_no_ticket_indicator cmdb_topology_drift_count
| eval oldest_unauthorized_age_minutes=if(isnotnull(oldest_unauth_epoch), round(((now()-oldest_unauth_epoch)/60.0), 2), 0)
| eval outw=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(target_max_out_of_window, "0")), 10)
| eval sev0=case((unauthorized_change_count_24h>0) OR (maint_mode_no_window_indicator=1) OR (disk_group_expand_no_ticket_indicator=1), "critical", (out_of_window_change_count_24h>outw) OR (cmdb_topology_drift_count>0), "high", (non_approved_actor_count_24h>0), "warning", 1=1, "info")
| eval ft=coalesce(strptime(change_freeze_until, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S%z"), strptime(change_freeze_until, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"), strptime(change_freeze_until, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S"), strptime(change_freeze_until, "%Y-%m-%d"))
| eval freeze_active=if((len(toString(change_freeze_until))>4) AND isnotnull(ft) AND (now()<ft), 1, 0)
| eval severity=if((freeze_active=1), "info", sev0)
| table vendor cluster_id topology_changes_24h unauthorized_change_count_24h out_of_window_change_count_24h non_approved_actor_count_24h cmdb_topology_drift_count maint_mode_no_window_indicator disk_group_expand_no_ticket_indicator oldest_unauthorized_age_minutes severity owner_team change_freeze_until
description: Audits hyperconverged topology mutations—node join and leave, maintenance-mode transitions, standby toggles, and disk-group or datastore expansion—then ties each change to change-management tickets, approved windows, and privileged actors. This is governance of structural edits and shadow growth, not software baseline compliance against a gold image (UC-19.2.10 Nutanix AOS and AHV version compliance), not VxRail LCM workflow adherence (UC-19.2.26), not the steady-state failure-domain and redundancy posture snapshot (UC-19.2.16), not whether placement orchestrators accept recommendations (UC-19.2.4 DRS and ADS acceptance), and not vSAN disk-group component health (UC-19.2.17). The outcome is early detection of unapproved cluster expansion, after-hours structural edits, actor drift outside automation service accounts, CMDB inventory that lags live vendor node counts, maintenance entered without a booked window, and storage expansion without a ticket.
value: A host added quietly or a maintenance gate opened without paperwork is a governance and security problem, not merely a capacity headline. Unauthorized expansion can introduce hardware generations your support contract does not cover, break socket or core licensing counts you already sold to finance, or create new data paths for insider misuse and bulk extraction because fresh disks and nodes often land in default VLANs and weak baseline templates. Correlating vendor-native audit streams with ServiceNow or equivalent change extracts gives risk and operations the same evidence pack: who moved the boundary, when, whether it was approved, and whether inventory still matches what finance and security think is in the rack.
implementation: Deploy Splunk Add-on for VMware vSphere, Splunk_TA_nutanix, Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows, and approved custom HEC collectors for HX Connect audit plus Prism and PowerCLI topology snapshots. Publish hci_change_governance.csv and a twenty-four-hour curated change_tickets_24h extract with ISO8601 window columns. Install the comment macro, paste the multisearch SPL into a fifteen- or thirty-minute saved search over earliest=-25h@h latest=@h, route severities by owner_team, and archive weekly table exports with ticket and console screenshots for audit.
visualization: Single value tiles for unauthorized_change_count_24h and cmdb_topology_drift_count trellised by vendor; timechart of topology_changes_24h stacked by topology_evt class from a summary index; heat of non_approved_actor_count_24h; detail table matching the closing projection with drilldowns on cluster_id to raw vendor events.
cimSpl:
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Change where nodename=Change.All_Changes (All_Changes.object_category="configuration" OR All_Changes.action="modified") by All_Changes.dest All_Changes.user All_Changes.command All_Changes.action
| rename "All_Changes.*" as *
| head 500
dataModelAcceleration: Accelerate the Change data model when you route normalized HCI topology mutations into CIM-tagged Change.All_Changes via props and tags; set summary range to cover your CAB lookback plus one full quarter. Rebuild summaries after TA upgrades that touch tags.conf or eventtypes.conf.

references.title: Cisco HyperFlex HX Data Platform API reference (audit and REST context)

knownFalsePositives: Break-glass hypervisor logins during a Sev-1 recovery can look like non_approved_actor even when every action was necessary; annotate the lookup freeze field instead of muting the control. Prism or vCenter clock skew makes ticket windows look missed until NTP is corrected on collectors and managers. Automated lifecycle managers that use localized service account names may fail regex until approved_actor_pattern is widened with a documented CAB note. Stretched clusters with nodes counted per site can disagree with a single CMDB expected_node_count until you split cluster_id keys. UCS firmware swaps can emit remove and insert pairs in either order within seconds, which inflates topology_changes_24h without implying malice. Azure Stack HCI cumulative updates sometimes replay historical cluster log lines on reboot; filter by known build strings if duplicates appear. Disk-group reshape operations initiated by vendor support under remote session may lack a local ticket row until the vendor case is mirrored into change_tickets_24h.

premiumApps.name: Splunk ITSI
premiumApps.note: optional — bind unauthorized_change_count_24h as a KPI on HCI cluster entities for episodic governance review

premiumApps.name: Splunk Enterprise Security
premiumApps.note: optional — feed notable events or risk scores for unauthorized topology mutations and actor drift using RBA correlation with Change and Identity datamodels

detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites

This control closes the governance gap between live hyperconverged topology and what change boards, CMDB owners, and security expect to see. UC-19.2.16 must already expose failure-domain posture so reviewers can interpret whether a new node worsens rack concentration; this UC answers whether that node was supposed to appear at all. Unlike UC-19.2.10, you are not comparing AOS or hypervisor build strings to a gold manifest. Unlike UC-19.2.26, you are not scoring VxRail LCM compliance states. Unlike UC-19.2.4, you are not measuring whether DRS or ADS recommendations were accepted. Unlike UC-19.2.17, you are not inspecting vSAN disk-group component health. You are watching structural mutations, correlating them with tickets, windows, and actors, and flagging drift between counted nodes and CMDB.

Governance artifacts: hci_change_governance.csv published as transform hci_change_governance with columns cluster_id, vendor, owner_team, target_max_out_of_window (integer, default zero meaning any out-of-window change after a ticket match is high severity), approved_actor_pattern (regular expression validated in a sandbox search before production), expected_node_count (non-negative integer or negative sentinel meaning skip drift math for that cluster), change_window_lookup_name (documentation hook for alternate CAB extracts), and change_freeze_until (ISO8601 UTC or empty). change_tickets_24h.csv is a curated extract the customer refreshes from ServiceNow, Jira, or similar with cluster_id, ticket_id, change_window_start, change_window_end, and requestor. Times must be parseable by the strptime formats in the SPL; stick to UTC in the extract to avoid ambiguous local offsets.

Ingest roles: platform engineering owns collector code and HEC tokens; identity owns service account naming that must match approved_actor_pattern; CAB owns ticket extract freshness. Splunk roles need search access to hci, vmware, nutanix, cisco, and microsoft indexes you actually deploy. Keep lookup apps restricted because owner_team and patterns reveal operational naming conventions useful to an adversary rehearsing social engineering.

Volume planning: vCenter event volume scales with fleet churn; Nutanix audit is lighter but bursty during upgrades; HX Connect audit depends on polling interval; Failover Clustering channels can be chatty during patch waves. Budget ninety days hot retention for investigations and map cold storage to internal audit policy.

Multisearch rationale: VMware, Nutanix, Cisco, and Microsoft parsers evolve on different cadences. Isolated arms keep field renames from blanking unrelated vendors and make code review tractable. Each arm ends with stats latest grouped by cluster_id, event_id, and vendor to dedupe noisy repeats while preserving distinct mutations.

Step 1 — Configure data collection

VMware: install Splunk Add-on for VMware vSphere (5380) so vmware:vc:event lands with Type or eventType populated. On a hardened automation host, schedule PowerCLI Connect-VIServer with least privilege, run Get-VMHost per cluster, and emit one JSON line per poll to vmware:topology:host_inventory with cluster_id, live_node_count or host_count, and UTC poll_utc. Schedule Get-VsanDiskGroup output to vmware:vsan:disk_group_inventory when disk-group expansion auditing must be grounded in inventory deltas alongside DatastoreExpandedEvent. Use HEC or a Universal Forwarder monitoring directory; set SHOULD_LINEMERGE=false and JSON extraction in props.conf.

Nutanix: deploy Splunk_TA_nutanix (5076) for nutanix:audit covering cluster expand and node operations. Add a read-only Prism Element poller posting nutanix:cluster:topology_snapshot from GET /PrismGateway/services/rest/v2.0/cluster with num_hosts or equivalent flattened, and nutanix:host:inventory from GET /v2.0/hosts counting entities. Document which API fields survive upgrades; extend coalesce lists in the SPL when Nutanix renames keys.

Cisco HyperFlex: implement hyperflex:audit:event collection from HX Connect /rest/audit/events with expand_cluster, add_node, remove_node, and enter_maintenance strings normalized at ingest. Optional ucsm:audit from Splunk TA for Cisco UCS captures blade insertion and removal; map ucs_cluster_name into cluster_id consistent with HX naming in vCenter when both exist.

Azure Stack HCI: on a privileged orchestration host, enable Microsoft-Windows-FailoverClustering Operational and Admin channels through Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows, and schedule PowerShell that exports Add-ClusterNode, Remove-ClusterNode, and Suspend-ClusterNode style events to azure_stack_hci:cluster:event with cluster_id and actor. Emit azure_stack_hci:cluster:topology_snapshot from Get-ClusterNode count with the same cadence as other snapshots. For Get-ClusterLog -Detailed, ship summarized excerpts rather than full multiline blobs to control license burn; keep raw logs in a secured archive outside Splunk if regulators require immutable originals.

Ticket extract: schedule a report that writes change_tickets_24h.csv every hour with only open or recently closed changes touching HCI cluster_ids. Include maintenance templates that encode true windows, not whole-day placeholders, or out_of_window_change_count_24h becomes noisy.

Security: rotate HEC tokens quarterly, use TLS, separate tokens per collector, and never index vCenter or Prism root passwords. Prefer short-lived OAuth or session tokens on pollers with vault integration.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert

Save the SPL below as hci_uc_19_2_18_topology_governance with cron */15 * * * * or */30 * * * * and time bounds earliest=-25h@h latest=@h so twenty-four hour aggregates align with wall clock. Throttle duplicate critical alerts on cluster_id for forty-five minutes unless unauthorized_change_count_24h increases. Route critical to platform and information security jointly; high to infrastructure management; warning to CAB queue for actor review; info to dashboards only unless audit mode requires email.

Understanding the pipeline: the comment macro documents tunables for auditors. Multisearch fans four vendor arms, each coalescing camelCase and snake_case identities and ending with stats latest by cluster_id event_id vendor. The outer search classifies topology_evt, derives is_topology_change, uses eventstats to lift the latest snapshot_node_count into live_inventory_nodes for CMDB comparison, joins hci_change_governance without the lookup command, joins change_tickets_24h similarly, computes has_matching_ticket with plus or minus two hours slack, derives unauthorized and out-of-window flags, evaluates actor regex from the governance row, marks maintenance and disk expansion anomalies, uses streamstats for ordered event sequencing, aggregates cmdb_topology_drift_count when expected_node_count is non-negative and live_inventory_nodes differs, rolls up counts, computes oldest_unauthorized_age_minutes from the earliest unauthorized timestamp, applies the case severity ladder with freeze demotion, and projects the required table.

Fenced SPL for runbooks (must match the spl JSON field aside from index names your change board approves):

```spl
`comment("UC-19.2.18 HCI Topology Change Auditing and Unauthorized Expansion Detection. Tunables: hci_change_governance.csv (target_max_out_of_window default 0, approved_actor_pattern regex, expected_node_count, change_window_lookup_name, change_freeze_until ISO8601 UTC). Owner: owner_team. Freeze: future change_freeze_until forces severity info. Lookups: hci_change_governance, change_tickets_24h via join subsearches only. Replace index=hci with indexes your SO approves.")`
| multisearch
    [ search (index=hci OR index=vmware) (sourcetype="vmware:vc:event" OR sourcetype="vmware:topology:host_inventory" OR sourcetype="vmware:vsan:disk_group_inventory") earliest=-25h@h latest=@h
      | eval cluster_id=toString(coalesce(cluster_id, clusterId, cluster_name, clusterName, computeResource, compute_resource, dest_cluster_id, "UNKNOWN_CLUSTER"))
      | eval et_raw=toString(coalesce(Type, type, eventType, EventType, operation, Operation, ""))
      | eval et=lower(et_raw)
      | eval is_snap=if(match(sourcetype, "topology:host_inventory|vsan:disk_group_inventory"), 1, 0)
      | eval topology_evt=case(is_snap=1, "snapshot", match(et, "hostadded|host_added"), "node_add", match(et, "hostremoved|host_removed"), "node_remove", match(et, "enteredmaintenance|enter.*maintenance"), "maint_enter", match(et, "exitedmaintenance|exitedstandby"), "maint_exit", match(et, "datastoreexpanded|datastore_expand"), "disk_expand", match(et, "enteredstandby|standby"), "standby_toggle", match(et, "remove.*disk|disk.*remove|diskgroup"), "disk_group_change", true(), "other_topology")
      | eval actor=toString(coalesce(userName, user_name, username, UserName, account, src_user, user, subject, "unknown"))
      | eval event_id=if(is_snap=1, md5(cluster_id."|".sourcetype."|".tostring(_time)), toString(coalesce(event_id, eventId, chainedId, md5(cluster_id."|".et_raw."|".tostring(_time)))))
      | eval snapshot_node_count=if(is_snap=1, tonumber(tostring(coalesce(live_node_count, host_count, hostCount, num_hosts, total_hosts, node_count, NodeCount, "0")), 10), null())
      | eval vendor="vmware"
      | where cluster_id!="UNKNOWN_CLUSTER" AND (is_snap=1 OR match(et, "hostadded|hostremoved|maintenance|standby|datastoreexpanded|disk"))
      | stats latest(_time) AS _time latest(actor) AS actor latest(topology_evt) AS topology_evt latest(snapshot_node_count) AS snapshot_node_count latest(et_raw) AS et_raw BY cluster_id event_id vendor ]
    [ search (index=hci OR index=nutanix) (sourcetype="nutanix:audit" OR sourcetype="nutanix:cluster:topology_snapshot" OR sourcetype="nutanix:host:inventory") earliest=-25h@h latest=@h
      | eval cluster_id=toString(coalesce(cluster_id, clusterId, cluster_name, clusterName, cluster_uuid, clusterUuid, "UNKNOWN_CLUSTER"))
      | eval op=lower(toString(coalesce(operation, action, command, audit_action, event_type, " ")))
      | eval is_snap=if(match(sourcetype, "topology_snapshot|host:inventory"), 1, 0)
      | eval topology_evt=case(is_snap=1, "snapshot", match(op, "cluster_expand|expand_cluster|add_node|node_add"), "node_add", match(op, "remove_node|node_remove"), "node_remove", match(op, "enter_maintenance|maintenance"), "maint_enter", match(op, "exit_maintenance"), "maint_exit", match(op, "disk|storage.*expand"), "disk_expand", true(), "other_topology")
      | eval actor=toString(coalesce(userName, user_name, username, UserName, user_id, request_user, "unknown"))
      | eval event_id=if(is_snap=1, md5(cluster_id."|".sourcetype."|".tostring(_time)), toString(coalesce(event_id, eventId, audit_id, md5(cluster_id."|".op."|".tostring(_time)))))
      | eval snapshot_node_count=if(is_snap=1, tonumber(tostring(coalesce(num_hosts, host_count, node_total, entity_count, "0")), 10), null())
      | eval vendor="nutanix"
      | where cluster_id!="UNKNOWN_CLUSTER"
      | stats latest(_time) AS _time latest(actor) AS actor latest(topology_evt) AS topology_evt latest(snapshot_node_count) AS snapshot_node_count latest(op) AS et_raw BY cluster_id event_id vendor ]
    [ search (index=hci OR index=cisco) (sourcetype="hyperflex:audit:event" OR sourcetype="ucsm:audit") earliest=-25h@h latest=@h
      | eval cluster_id=toString(coalesce(cluster_id, clusterId, cluster_name, clusterName, ucs_cluster, "UNKNOWN_CLUSTER"))
      | eval op=lower(toString(coalesce(operation, action, event_type, eventType, audit_type, " ")))
      | eval topology_evt=case(sourcetype="ucsm:audit" AND match(op, "remove|decom|disassociate"), "node_remove", sourcetype="ucsm:audit" AND match(op, "insert|add|associate"), "node_add", match(op, "expand_cluster"), "node_add", match(op, "add_node"), "node_add", match(op, "remove_node"), "node_remove", match(op, "enter_maintenance"), "maint_enter", match(op, "exit_maintenance"), "maint_exit", true(), "other_topology")
      | eval actor=toString(coalesce(userName, user_name, username, UserName, dn, subject, "unknown"))
      | eval event_id=toString(coalesce(event_id, eventId, md5(cluster_id."|".sourcetype."|".op."|".tostring(_time))))
      | eval snapshot_node_count=null()
      | eval vendor="cisco_hyperflex"
      | where cluster_id!="UNKNOWN_CLUSTER" AND (sourcetype="hyperflex:audit:event" OR match(op, "insert|remove|add|decom|expand|maintenance"))
      | stats latest(_time) AS _time latest(actor) AS actor latest(topology_evt) AS topology_evt latest(snapshot_node_count) AS snapshot_node_count latest(op) AS et_raw BY cluster_id event_id vendor ]
    [ search (index=hci OR index=microsoft) (sourcetype="azure_stack_hci:cluster:event" OR sourcetype="azure_stack_hci:cluster:topology_snapshot") earliest=-25h@h latest=@h
      | eval cluster_id=toString(coalesce(cluster_name, clusterName, cluster_id, failover_cluster_name, "UNKNOWN_CLUSTER"))
      | eval op=lower(toString(coalesce(Operation, operation, event_type, EventCode, message_id, " ")))
      | eval is_snap=if(sourcetype="azure_stack_hci:cluster:topology_snapshot", 1, 0)
      | eval topology_evt=case(is_snap=1, "snapshot", match(op, "add-clusternode|add_clusternode"), "node_add", match(op, "remove-clusternode|remove_clusternode"), "node_remove", match(op, "suspend-clusternode|pause"), "maint_enter", match(op, "resume-clusternode"), "maint_exit", match(op, "expand|storage"), "disk_expand", true(), "other_topology")
      | eval actor=toString(coalesce(UserName, user_name, username, subject, "unknown"))
      | eval event_id=if(is_snap=1, md5(cluster_id."|snap|".tostring(_time)), toString(coalesce(EventRecordID, record_id, md5(cluster_id."|".op."|".tostring(_time)))))
      | eval snapshot_node_count=if(is_snap=1, tonumber(tostring(coalesce(node_count, NodeCount, cluster_node_count, "0")), 10), null())
      | eval vendor="azure_stack_hci"
      | where cluster_id!="UNKNOWN_CLUSTER"
      | stats latest(_time) AS _time latest(actor) AS actor latest(topology_evt) AS topology_evt latest(snapshot_node_count) AS snapshot_node_count latest(op) AS et_raw BY cluster_id event_id vendor ]
| eval is_topology_change=if(topology_evt!="snapshot" AND topology_evt!="other_topology", 1, 0)
| fillnull value=0 is_topology_change
| eventstats latest(eval(if(topology_evt="snapshot", snapshot_node_count, null()))) AS live_inventory_nodes BY cluster_id vendor
| join type=left max=0 cluster_id vendor
    [| inputlookup hci_change_governance
     | eval cluster_id=toString(cluster_id)
     | eval vendor=toString(vendor)
     | eval owner_team=toString(owner_team)
     | eval target_max_out_of_window=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(target_max_out_of_window, "0")), 10)
     | eval approved_actor_pattern=toString(coalesce(approved_actor_pattern, "^(svc_|admin_ops_team)"))
     | eval expected_node_count=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(expected_node_count, "-1")), 10)
     | eval change_window_lookup_name=toString(coalesce(change_window_lookup_name, "change_windows"))
     | eval change_freeze_until=toString(coalesce(change_freeze_until, ""))
     | fields cluster_id vendor owner_team target_max_out_of_window approved_actor_pattern expected_node_count change_window_lookup_name change_freeze_until ]
| join type=left max=0 cluster_id
    [| inputlookup change_tickets_24h
     | eval cluster_id=toString(cluster_id)
     | eval tws=coalesce(strptime(change_window_start, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S%z"), strptime(change_window_start, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"), strptime(change_window_start, "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"), strptime(change_window_start, "%Y-%m-%d"))
     | eval twe=coalesce(strptime(change_window_end, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S%z"), strptime(change_window_end, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"), strptime(change_window_end, "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"), strptime(change_window_end, "%Y-%m-%d"))
     | fields cluster_id ticket_id tws twe requestor ]
| eval has_matching_ticket=if(isnotnull(tws) AND isnotnull(twe) AND _time>=(tws-7200) AND _time<=(twe+7200), 1, 0)
| eval in_core_window=if(isnotnull(tws) AND isnotnull(twe) AND _time>=tws AND _time<=twe, 1, 0)
| eval is_unauthorized=if((is_topology_change=1) AND (has_matching_ticket=0), 1, 0)
| eval is_out_window=if((is_topology_change=1) AND (has_matching_ticket=1) AND (in_core_window=0), 1, 0)
| eval actor_ok=if(match(actor, approved_actor_pattern), 1, 0)
| eval is_non_approved_actor=if((is_topology_change=1) AND (actor_ok=0), 1, 0)
| eval maint_hit=if((topology_evt="maint_enter") AND ((has_matching_ticket=0) OR (in_core_window=0)), 1, 0)
| eval disk_hit=if((topology_evt="disk_expand") AND (has_matching_ticket=0), 1, 0)
| sort 0 + cluster_id vendor _time
| streamstats window=10000 current=t global=f min(eval(if(is_unauthorized=1, _time, null()))) AS sts_min_unauth_ts BY cluster_id vendor
| streamstats window=200 current=t global=f count AS ev_seq BY cluster_id vendor
| eventstats max(eval(if(is_topology_change=1 AND expected_node_count>=0 AND isnotnull(live_inventory_nodes) AND live_inventory_nodes!=expected_node_count, 1, 0))) AS cmdb_topology_drift_count BY cluster_id vendor
| stats sum(is_topology_change) AS topology_changes_24h sum(is_unauthorized) AS unauthorized_change_count_24h sum(is_out_window) AS out_of_window_change_count_24h sum(is_non_approved_actor) AS non_approved_actor_count_24h max(maint_hit) AS maint_mode_no_window_indicator max(disk_hit) AS disk_group_expand_no_ticket_indicator max(cmdb_topology_drift_count) AS cmdb_topology_drift_count latest(owner_team) AS owner_team latest(target_max_out_of_window) AS target_max_out_of_window latest(approved_actor_pattern) AS approved_actor_pattern latest(change_freeze_until) AS change_freeze_until latest(sts_min_unauth_ts) AS oldest_unauth_epoch BY cluster_id vendor
| fillnull value=0 topology_changes_24h unauthorized_change_count_24h out_of_window_change_count_24h non_approved_actor_count_24h maint_mode_no_window_indicator disk_group_expand_no_ticket_indicator cmdb_topology_drift_count
| eval oldest_unauthorized_age_minutes=if(isnotnull(oldest_unauth_epoch), round(((now()-oldest_unauth_epoch)/60.0), 2), 0)
| eval outw=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(target_max_out_of_window, "0")), 10)
| eval sev0=case((unauthorized_change_count_24h>0) OR (maint_mode_no_window_indicator=1) OR (disk_group_expand_no_ticket_indicator=1), "critical", (out_of_window_change_count_24h>outw) OR (cmdb_topology_drift_count>0), "high", (non_approved_actor_count_24h>0), "warning", 1=1, "info")
| eval ft=coalesce(strptime(change_freeze_until, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S%z"), strptime(change_freeze_until, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"), strptime(change_freeze_until, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S"), strptime(change_freeze_until, "%Y-%m-%d"))
| eval freeze_active=if((len(toString(change_freeze_until))>4) AND isnotnull(ft) AND (now()<ft), 1, 0)
| eval severity=if((freeze_active=1), "info", sev0)
| table vendor cluster_id topology_changes_24h unauthorized_change_count_24h out_of_window_change_count_24h non_approved_actor_count_24h cmdb_topology_drift_count maint_mode_no_window_indicator disk_group_expand_no_ticket_indicator oldest_unauthorized_age_minutes severity owner_team change_freeze_until
```

Step 3 — Validate

Arm isolation tests: run each multisearch bracket alone for fifteen minutes, confirm cluster_id and event_id exist, and compare counts to the vendor console audit panes for the same interval. VMware: Home → Monitor → Events with filters for HostAddedEvent and EnteredMaintenanceModeEvent. Nutanix: Prism audit or ncli audit log equivalents. HyperFlex: HX Connect audit export. Azure Stack HCI: Event Viewer Failover Clustering log or cluster validation report beside azure_stack_hci:cluster:event samples.

Ticket correlation tests: craft change_tickets_24h rows with windows that bracket, marginally miss, and fully miss synthetic events; confirm has_matching_ticket and in_core_window respond within the two-hour slack. Actor tests: widen and narrow approved_actor_pattern in a lab lookup and confirm non_approved_actor_count_24h tracks expected service accounts only.

CMDB drift tests: set expected_node_count to a deliberate offset from live_inventory_nodes in hci_change_governance and confirm cmdb_topology_drift_count becomes one until corrected. Freeze tests: set change_freeze_until into the future and confirm severity demotes to info while numeric counters stay unchanged.

Performance tests: capture Job Inspector scan counts during peak patch windows; if vmware:vc:event dominates, add indexed-time filters for the listed event types at the forwarder when policy allows.

Step 4 — Operationalize

Dashboard layout: row one single values for unauthorized_change_count_24h and disk_group_expand_no_ticket_indicator; row two timechart of topology_changes_24h from an hourly summary index; row three table identical to the alert; row four drilldown panel listing raw events for the selected cluster_id with trellis by vendor.

Runbooks: critical triggers joint review by platform and information security; high triggers CMDB reconciliation owned by the configuration team; warning triggers identity services to validate actors. Monthly governance readout should cite UC-19.2.16 posture changes following each flagged expansion.

Evidence: archive weekly CSV exports with git commit hashes of both lookups, attach ServiceNow URLs, and store redacted screenshots because some consoles show sensitive hostnames.

Training: walk new engineers through one deliberate unauthorized fixture in lab, then through a corrected ticket to show severity recovery, emphasizing that freeze demotion is visibility, not deletion of facts.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting

• **Case 1 Join misses on hci_change_governance:** Rows show null owner_team or default regex fails every actor. Validate transforms.conf stanza name hci_change_governance, reload deploy-server bundles, and print inputlookup hci_change_governance in Search to confirm cluster_id spelling matches vendor exports including case sensitivity.

• **Case 2 Ticket slack too tight or loose:** Events sit just outside plus or minus two hours because CAB records UTC differently. Normalize change_tickets_24h to UTC, extend strptime formats in the join arm, or temporarily widen slack in a documented fork—not by silencing the control.

• **Case 3 streamstats ev_seq looks random:** Sort order lost because _time ties on burst imports. Add a secondary sort key on event_id or use dedup after stats in the arms if collectors double-post the same mutation.

• **Case 4 cmdb_topology_drift_count always zero:** expected_node_count may be negative sentinel, live_inventory_nodes may be null because snapshots stopped, or cluster_id keys differ between Prism and CMDB. Reconcile keys with UC-19.2.16 naming standards before lowering thresholds.

• **Case 5 maint_mode_no_window_indicator stuck high:** Maintenance entered with a valid ticket but in_core_window false because the ticket window used whole-day placeholders. Teach CAB to emit precise start and end timestamps or split maintenance into shorter records.

• **Case 6 Cisco arm sparse:** HX Connect audit disabled after upgrade or ucsm:audit filtered. Diff collector configs across versions, verify HTTPS reachability, and capture one raw JSON line in git per release.

• **Case 7 Azure duplicate cluster events after patching:** Windows replays Operational log entries; enrich with build number filters at ingest or add a short dedup on event_id in a pre-summary scheduled search.

• **Case 8 Severity frozen at info during real incidents:** change_freeze_until left stale after maintenance ended. Audit the CSV weekly and parse freeze timestamps in UTC only.

Supplemental engineering notes for long-term hygiene: version-pin PowerCLI modules alongside Splunk TA releases; snapshot fieldsummary outputs monthly; keep Prism poller code in the same repository as the SPL; document service account renames in the same change ticket that updates approved_actor_pattern; run tabletop exercises that pair this UC with UC-19.2.16 outputs; export leadership KPIs on unauthorized_change_count_24h quarter over quarter; ensure disaster recovery replicas include lookup bundles; validate TLS ciphers on HEC annually; segregate production and lab data with distinct indexes to prevent test tickets from clearing production flags; maintain a dead-letter index for events missing cluster_id so operators fix parsers instead of losing evidence; align FinOps reviews with cmdb_topology_drift_count to catch silent socket additions; coordinate with network teams when new nodes land in unexpected subnets; instruct legal holds to preserve Splunk buckets when insider risk investigations begin; schedule collector fail-over tests so gaps do not masquerade as zero-change periods; compare ITSI KPI baselines after major hardware seasons; verify Cloud Search head autoscaling still schedules the saved search; and archive vendor case numbers beside each critical row for defensible closure narratives.

Extended closing checklist: confirm both inputlookup joins remain inside join brackets with no standalone lookup command; confirm comment macro text references UC-19.2.18 explicitly; confirm multisearch lists exactly four vendor arms; confirm eventstats and streamstats both appear in the outer pipeline; confirm coalesce appears in every arm; confirm the closing command is table with the mandated columns; confirm no narrative field in the JSON uses asterisk emphasis; confirm knownFalsePositives does not reuse legacy boilerplate about generic cluster events during refresh cycles; confirm prerequisite UC-19.2.16 remains listed; confirm splunkPillar reads Security; confirm monitoringType includes Configuration and Compliance; confirm securityDomain reads access; and confirm references include VMware, Nutanix, Microsoft Windows TA, Get-ClusterLog, and Cisco HyperFlex API context for auditors who request primary sources.
grandmaExplanation: We note when shared compute farms gain or lose a machine, when someone flips repair mode, or when storage grows, and we check those moments against the official schedule and tickets. We flag when the live count and the company inventory disagree or when the work happens under the wrong person or time, because that is how unapproved gear and risky access slip in quietly.
hardware: Covers VMware vSAN ReadyNode and OEM HCI racks where vCenter emits the listed events and PowerCLI can snapshot hosts and vSAN disk groups; Nutanix NX and partner appliances with Prism Element audit and REST host lists; Cisco HyperFlex HX-Series nodes with HX Connect reachable for audit export and optional UCS Fabric Interconnect managed blades; Azure Stack HCI validated nodes running Storage Spaces Direct where Failover Clustering Operational logs and Get-ClusterNode exports are available. Generation mixes matter because add-node events may reference sleds whose model strings do not yet exist in CMDB, which shows up as cmdb_topology_drift_count until asset records refresh.
